free_text: Activation cross sections and isomeric cross section
ratios for 184Os(n,2n)183m,gOs, 190Os(n,p)190m,gRe
and 86Sr(n,2n)85m,gSr reactions from 13.5 to 14.8 MeV
Department of Physics, Hexi University,
Zhangye 734000
Cross sections for
93Nb(n,2n)92mNb reaction were selected as monitors to
measure the reaction cross section on several Os
isotopes.
The neutron energies in these measurements were
determined by cross section ratios for the
90Zr(n,2n)89m+gZr and 93Nb(n,2n)92mNb reactions
- cross sections for
normalization.
The neutron energies
were determined.
The gamma-ray activity of 183mOs, 183gOs,
190mRe, 190gRe, and 92mNb were determined by a
high-purity germanium (HPGe) detector (ORTEC,
model GEM 60P, Crystal diameter: 70.1 mm, Crystal
length: 72.3 mm) with a relative efficiency of ~68 %
and an energy  resolution of 1.69 keV at1332 keV for
60Co. The efficiency of the detector was pre-calibrated
using various standard gamma-sources.
The gamma-ray activities of 85mSr, 85gSr and
92mNb were determined by a CH8403 coaxial high-purity
germanium detector (sensitive volume 110 cm3) (made
in the People's Republic of China) with a relative
efficiency of 20% and an energy resolution of 3 keV at
1.33 MeV. The efficiency of the detector was
precalibrated using various standard gamma-sources.
The main error sources are:
* counting statistics,
* standard cross section,
* detector efficiency,
* weight of samples,
* self-absorption of gamma-rays and the coincidence
sum effect of cascade gamma-rays,
* measurement geometry.
The total uncertainty in each cross section was
obtained by combining the individual uncertainties
in quadrature.
scale uncertainty
Data are from the tables of the ref.
Entry approved by Dr. Junhua Luo
SD
VS. decay data corrected in 007,010.
Irradiation of was carried out at
the K-400 Neutron Generator at Chinese Academy of
Engineering Physics (CAEP) and lasted 2 to 3 h with
a yield ~4 to 5 x 10**10 n/s.
Approximately 2 g of high-purity Os powder (>99.95%)
was pressed into a pellet of 20 mm diameter. Thin
niobium neutron flux monitor foils of the same
diameter as the sample were then attached in front
and at the back of each sample.
Neutrons were produced by the T(d,n)4He
reaction with an effective deuteron beam energy of
134 keV and beam current of 230 mu-A. The tritium-
titanium (T-Ti) target used in the generator was
2.18 mg/cm2 thick. The neutron flux was monitored by
a uranium fission chamber so that corrections could
be made for small variations in the yield.

Approximately 3 g of high-purity SrCO3 powder (>99.9%)
was pressed into a pellet of 20 mm diameter.
Thin niobium neutron flux monitor foils of
the same diameter as the sample were then attached
in front and at the back of each sample.
Neutrons were produced by the T(d,n)4He
reaction with an effective deuteron beam energy of
135 keV, and a beam current of 500 mu-A. The solid
tritium-titanium (T-Ti) target used in the generator
was about 1.35 mg/cm2 thick. The neutron was
monitored by the accompanying alpha-particles so
fluence rate that corrections could be made for
small variations in the yield.
